  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Operations Supervisor / Assistant Manager",
  "description": "Lazeto Hotels is a newly established four star hotel located in the Zango area of Kogi, Lokoja Nigeria.The hotel boasts 11 elegantly designed rooms,a roof top bar, restuarant,gym,swimming pool,fish pond.Strategically positioned to cater to business travellers,tourists and locals.Lazeto hotels aims to provide exceptional hospitality services in the confluence city.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Supervisor / Assistant Manager

Job Location: Zango, Kogi
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Supervise the daily operations of all hotel departments.
	Ensure excellent guest service and promptly resolve guest concerns.
	Monitor staff performance and enforce company policies and standards.
	Coordinate with department heads to ensure smooth hotel operations.
	Prepare operational reports and monitor departmental performance.
	Support staff scheduling, training, and performance management.
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, and quality standards.
	Assist management in achieving occupancy, revenue, and operational targets.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or HND in Hospitality Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in hotel operations or hospitality management.
	Strong leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent organizational and multitasking abilities.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and hotel management systems is an advantage
	Applicants should reside aroud Zango and environs.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: Lazetohotels@gmail.com using &ldquo;Operations Supervisor/Assistant Manager&quot; as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.",

Operations Supervisor / Assistant Manager Job at Lazeto Hotels




        
    

        
        Lazeto Hotels is a newly established four star hotel located in the Zango area of Kogi, Lokoja Nigeria.The hotel boasts 11 elegantly designed rooms,a roof top bar, restuarant,gym,swimming pool,fish pond.Strategically positioned to cater to business travellers,tourists and locals.Lazeto hotels aims to provide exceptional hospitality services in the confluence city.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Supervisor / Assistant Manager

Job Location: Zango, Kogi
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Supervise the daily operations of all hotel departments.
	Ensure excellent guest service and promptly resolve guest concerns.
	Monitor staff performance and enforce company policies and standards.
	Coordinate with department heads to ensure smooth hotel operations.
	Prepare operational reports and monitor departmental performance.
	Support staff scheduling, training, and performance management.
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, and quality standards.
	Assist management in achieving occupancy, revenue, and operational targets.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree or HND in Hospitality Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in hotel operations or hospitality management.
	Strong leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent organizational and multitasking abilities.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and hotel management systems is an advantage
	Applicants should reside aroud Zango and environs.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: [email protected] using “Operations Supervisor/Assistant Manager" as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Founded by Amina Aminu Gamagira, the brand is celebrated for crafting elegant traditional and contemporary outfits, including kaftans, bubus, and custom gowns.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Embroidery Designer

Job Location: Kano

Minimum qualifications


	SSCE
	Proven experience in embroidery design
	Proficiency in using embroidery machines and software
	Ability to create unique and high-quality designs
	Strong attention to detail and creativity
	Ability to work efficiently and meet deadlines
	Professional attitude and excellent work ethic
	Good team spirit and willingness to learn.


Benefits


	Accomodation available.
	Opportunity for growth.
	Professional work environment.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: mienahcouture@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: For more enquiries, Call / WhatsApp: 08164566443.",

Embroidery Designer Job at Mienah Couture




        
    

        
        Mienah Couture is a prominent Nigerian fashion brand specializing in bespoke tailoring, ready-to-wear (RTW) garments, and intricate hand beading and embroidery. Founded by Amina Aminu Gamagira, the brand is celebrated for crafting elegant traditional and contemporary outfits, including kaftans, bubus, and custom gowns.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Embroidery Designer

Job Location: Kano

Minimum qualifications
     

            
    




	SSCE
	Proven experience in embroidery design
	Proficiency in using embroidery machines and software
	Ability to create unique and high-quality designs
	Strong attention to detail and creativity
	Ability to work efficiently and meet deadlines
	Professional attitude and excellent work ethic
	Good team spirit and willingness to learn.


Benefits


	Accomodation available.
	Opportunity for growth.
	Professional work environment.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: For more enquiries, Call / WhatsApp: 08164566443.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Hotel Receptionist",
  "description": "Lazeto hotels is a newly established four star hotel located in the Zango area of Kogi, Lokoja Nigeria.The hotel boasts 11 elegantly designed rooms,a roof top bar, restuarant,gym,swimming pool,fish pond.Strategically positioned to cater to business travellers,tourists and locals.Lazeto hotels aims to provide exceptional hospitality services in the confluence city.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Hotel Receptionist

Job Location:&nbsp;&nbsp;Zango, Kogi
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Welcome and check in/check out guests.
	Handle reservations, inquiries, and guest requests.
	Answer phone calls and respond to emails professionally.
	Process payments and maintain accurate guest records.
	Coordinate with Housekeeping and other departments to ensure guest satisfaction.
	Resolve guest complaints promptly and courteously.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND/HND/B.Sc.
	Previous experience in a hotel or customer service role is an advantage.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Basic computer knowledge and ability to use hotel management software.
	Well-groomed, organized, and able to work shifts, including weekends and public holidays.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: Lazetohotels@gmail.com using &ldquo;Application for Hotel Receptionist&quot; as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-27",
  "validThrough": "2026-07-04",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",

Hotel Receptionist Job at Lazeto hotels




        
    

        
        Lazeto hotels is a newly established four star hotel located in the Zango area of Kogi, Lokoja Nigeria.The hotel boasts 11 elegantly designed rooms,a roof top bar, restuarant,gym,swimming pool,fish pond.Strategically positioned to cater to business travellers,tourists and locals.Lazeto hotels aims to provide exceptional hospitality services in the confluence city.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Hotel Receptionist

Job Location:  Zango, Kogi
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Welcome and check in/check out guests.
	Handle reservations, inquiries, and guest requests.
	Answer phone calls and respond to emails professionally.
	Process payments and maintain accurate guest records.
	Coordinate with Housekeeping and other departments to ensure guest satisfaction.
	Resolve guest complaints promptly and courteously.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND/HND/B.Sc.
	Previous experience in a hotel or customer service role is an advantage.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Basic computer knowledge and ability to use hotel management software.
	Well-groomed, organized, and able to work shifts, including weekends and public holidays.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “Application for Hotel Receptionist" as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Project Manager",
  "description": "O.K. Isokariari (Nigeria) Limited is a leading Engineering, Procurement, and Construction company with over 50 years of robust experience in civil engineering and building projects. Established in 1970 and incorporated in 1972, the company is recognized for its efficiency, reliability, and value-added services for both government agencies and private clients. Renowned for completing heavy civil engineering projects within budget and on schedule, the company ensures high-quality standards and a commitment to Health, Safety, and Environmental protection. With state-of-the-art equipment including asphalt and mixing plants, quarry operations, and transport fleets the company successfully executes complex infrastructure projects like highways, bridges, and residential areas in compliance with international standards.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Project Manager

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The Project Manager will be responsible for the end-to-end management of construction projects, ensuring that all building and road infrastructure works are executed to the highest engineering standards.
	The ideal candidate will bridge the gap between design and physical delivery, overseeing site teams, managing resources, and ensuring strict adherence to budget, quality, and health and safety (HSE) protocols.


Responsibilities


	Develop comprehensive project plans, schedules, and budgets, ensuring all milestones are met on time.
	Oversee site preparation, earthworks, drainage systems, and structural development for both buildings and road construction.
	Interpret technical drawings, blueprints, and specifications to direct field teams effectively.
	Manage the procurement and deployment of heavy machinery, materials, and labor to maximize site efficiency.
	Direct and mentor site supervisors, engineers, and technical staff to ensure high-performance standards.
	Monitor daily site progress and provide accurate, detailed reports to senior management.
	Act as the primary liaison between the company, clients, and relevant regulatory agencies.
	Manage contract administration, including tracking variations, project costs, and document control.
	Ensure clear communication between project sites and the head office, specifically regarding logistics and payroll.


Requirements


	Professional Certification: Current and active registration with the Council for the Regulation of Engineering in Nigeria (COREN) is mandatory. Professional membership in the Nigerian Society of Engineers (NSE) is required.
	Experience: A minimum of 15 years of progressive experience in the construction industry, with a proven track record of successfully delivering large-scale road infrastructure and commercial building projects.
	Proficiency in project management software (e.g., MS Project, Primavera) and AutoCAD.
	Advanced knowledge of modern construction materials and earthmoving equipment.
	Strong analytical skills for reporting, scheduling, and budget management.
	Proven ability to lead diverse teams, resolve technical disputes on-site, and manage high-pressure situations under strict deadlines.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hr@okisokariari.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Project Manager Job at O.K. Isokariari & Sons (Nigeria) Limited




        
    

        
        
     

            
    

O.K. Isokariari (Nigeria) Limited is a leading Engineering, Procurement, and Construction company with over 50 years of robust experience in civil engineering and building projects. Established in 1970 and incorporated in 1972, the company is recognized for its efficiency, reliability, and value-added services for both government agencies and private clients. Renowned for completing heavy civil engineering projects within budget and on schedule, the company ensures high-quality standards and a commitment to Health, Safety, and Environmental protection. With state-of-the-art equipment including asphalt and mixing plants, quarry operations, and transport fleets the company successfully executes complex infrastructure projects like highways, bridges, and residential areas in compliance with international standards.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Project Manager

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The Project Manager will be responsible for the end-to-end management of construction projects, ensuring that all building and road infrastructure works are executed to the highest engineering standards.
	The ideal candidate will bridge the gap between design and physical delivery, overseeing site teams, managing resources, and ensuring strict adherence to budget, quality, and health and safety (HSE) protocols.


Responsibilities


	Develop comprehensive project plans, schedules, and budgets, ensuring all milestones are met on time.
	Oversee site preparation, earthworks, drainage systems, and structural development for both buildings and road construction.
	Interpret technical drawings, blueprints, and specifications to direct field teams effectively.
	Manage the procurement and deployment of heavy machinery, materials, and labor to maximize site efficiency.
	Direct and mentor site supervisors, engineers, and technical staff to ensure high-performance standards.
	Monitor daily site progress and provide accurate, detailed reports to senior management.
	Act as the primary liaison between the company, clients, and relevant regulatory agencies.
	Manage contract administration, including tracking variations, project costs, and document control.
	Ensure clear communication between project sites and the head office, specifically regarding logistics and payroll.


Requirements


	Professional Certification: Current and active registration with the Council for the Regulation of Engineering in Nigeria (COREN) is mandatory. Professional membership in the Nigerian Society of Engineers (NSE) is required.
	Experience: A minimum of 15 years of progressive experience in the construction industry, with a proven track record of successfully delivering large-scale road infrastructure and commercial building projects.
	Proficiency in project management software (e.g., MS Project, Primavera) and AutoCAD.
	Advanced knowledge of modern construction materials and earthmoving equipment.
	Strong analytical skills for reporting, scheduling, and budget management.
	Proven ability to lead diverse teams, resolve technical disputes on-site, and manage high-pressure situations under strict deadlines.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Clinical Psychologist",
  "description": "Intersect Consortium, a consortium of Mental Health Providers, is the largest provider of mental health services in West Africa. Our Members understand the growing need for Mental Health Services and Products in Africa and continually work to provide Awareness, Education, Treatment Support, and technological services to Individuals, Companies, Families, and society at large. Olive Prime Psychological services a subsidiary, provides patient centred mental health care in a calm and serene environment with a robust mixture of locally and internationally trained professionals.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Clinical Psychologist

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a licensed and experienced Clinical Psychologist to join our multidisciplinary team of mental health professionals working in a treatment centre that offers inpatient and outpatient services.


Job Description


	Provide psychological assessment and consultation services
	Diagnose psychological, emotional or behavioral disorders of patients and develop treatment plan
	Provide therapy to groups and individuals
	Prospect for new clients by networking, and using other acceptable means of generating interest from potential clients and ultimately converting them into clients.
	Develop and implement individual treatment plan for different age groups (children inclusive)
	Identify innovative tactics, corresponding business plans and sustainable operations to drive revenue and volume growth with a bid to grow business.
	Work with multidisciplinary team alongside doctors, nurses and social workers, psychiatrists and occupational therapists
	Facilitate diverse group therapeutic sessions
	Counsel individuals and groups regarding problems such as stress, substance abuse, and family situations, in order to modify behavior.
	Modify treatment activities or approaches as needed to comply with changes in clients&rsquo; status
	Guide clients in the development of their skills or strategies for dealing with their problems
	Have an understanding of Bio Psychosocial Approach for assessment and treatment plan for every patient.
	Select, administer, score, and interpret psychological tests in order to obtain information on individuals&#39; intelligence, achievements, interests, and personalities
	Be conversant with different treatment methods like cognitive behavioral therapy, dialectic behavioral therapy, talk therapy, person centered therapy etc.
	Be conversant with the electronic medical record system for proper documentation of progress notes and treatment plan.
	Evaluate clients&rsquo; physical or mental condition based on review of client information
	Collaborate with other staff members to perform clinical assessments or develop treatment plans
	Refer patients, clients or family members to community resources or to specialists as necessary
	Act as client advocates to coordinate required services or to resolve emergency problems in crisis situations
	Coordinate family inclusive therapy to assist them in understanding, dealing with or supporting patients
	Evaluate the effectiveness of counselling programs on clients&rsquo; progress in resolving identified problems and moving towards defined objectives
	Meet with families, probation officers, police or other interested parties to exchange necessary information during the treatment process
	Plan, organize or lead structured programs of counselling, work, study, recreation or social activities for clients
	Plan or conduct programs to prevent substance abuse or improve community health or counselling services
	Psycho educate client on the importance of medication compliance.
	Learn about new developments in counselling by reading professional literature, attending courses and seminars or establishing and maintain contact with other social services agencies
	Gather information about community mental health needs or resources that could be used in conjunction with therapy
	Have an understanding of neuron developmental pathways, how to diagnose, carry out assessments and draw up appropriate treatment plan to meet specific needs.
	Ensure that after discharge client is transferred to a robust aftercare plan for proper follow up and management.


Requirements


	A minimum of Master&#39;s Degree in Clinical Psychology
	Minimum of 2 years Clinical experience
	Business Acumen and Business development experience
	Strong verbal and written communication skill
	Proactive problem-solving approach
	High level of professionalism
	Ability to uphold strict confidentiality
	Ability to observe, assess, and record symptoms, reactions, and progress.
	Knowledge of crisis intervention techniques.
	Ability to effectively communicate medical information, test results, diagnoses and/or proposed treatment in a manner easily understood by the client.
	Interviewing and psychological/developmental evaluation skills
	Knowledge of psychological test administration, scoring, and interpretation.
	Ability to maintain emotional stability to cope with human suffering, emergencies, and other stresses.


Salary
N200,000.00 - N250,000.00 / Month (Gross)

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: hr@intersectconsortium.com using the Job Position and location as the subject of the email.",

Clinical Psychologist Job at Intersect Consortium




        
    

        
        Intersect Consortium, a consortium of Mental Health Providers, is the largest provider of mental health services in West Africa. Our Members understand the growing need for Mental Health Services and Products in Africa and continually work to provide Awareness, Education, Treatment Support, and technological services to Individuals, Companies, Families, and society at large. Olive Prime Psychological services a subsidiary, provides patient centred mental health care in a calm and serene environment with a robust mixture of locally and internationally trained professionals.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Clinical Psychologist

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time
     

            
    



Job Summary


	We are seeking a licensed and experienced Clinical Psychologist to join our multidisciplinary team of mental health professionals working in a treatment centre that offers inpatient and outpatient services.


Job Description


	Provide psychological assessment and consultation services
	Diagnose psychological, emotional or behavioral disorders of patients and develop treatment plan
	Provide therapy to groups and individuals
	Prospect for new clients by networking, and using other acceptable means of generating interest from potential clients and ultimately converting them into clients.
	Develop and implement individual treatment plan for different age groups (children inclusive)
	Identify innovative tactics, corresponding business plans and sustainable operations to drive revenue and volume growth with a bid to grow business.
	Work with multidisciplinary team alongside doctors, nurses and social workers, psychiatrists and occupational therapists
	Facilitate diverse group therapeutic sessions
	Counsel individuals and groups regarding problems such as stress, substance abuse, and family situations, in order to modify behavior.
	Modify treatment activities or approaches as needed to comply with changes in clients’ status
	Guide clients in the development of their skills or strategies for dealing with their problems
	Have an understanding of Bio Psychosocial Approach for assessment and treatment plan for every patient.
	Select, administer, score, and interpret psychological tests in order to obtain information on individuals' intelligence, achievements, interests, and personalities
	Be conversant with different treatment methods like cognitive behavioral therapy, dialectic behavioral therapy, talk therapy, person centered therapy etc.
	Be conversant with the electronic medical record system for proper documentation of progress notes and treatment plan.
	Evaluate clients’ physical or mental condition based on review of client information
	Collaborate with other staff members to perform clinical assessments or develop treatment plans
	Refer patients, clients or family members to community resources or to specialists as necessary
	Act as client advocates to coordinate required services or to resolve emergency problems in crisis situations
	Coordinate family inclusive therapy to assist them in understanding, dealing with or supporting patients
	Evaluate the effectiveness of counselling programs on clients’ progress in resolving identified problems and moving towards defined objectives
	Meet with families, probation officers, police or other interested parties to exchange necessary information during the treatment process
	Plan, organize or lead structured programs of counselling, work, study, recreation or social activities for clients
	Plan or conduct programs to prevent substance abuse or improve community health or counselling services
	Psycho educate client on the importance of medication compliance.
	Learn about new developments in counselling by reading professional literature, attending courses and seminars or establishing and maintain contact with other social services agencies
	Gather information about community mental health needs or resources that could be used in conjunction with therapy
	Have an understanding of neuron developmental pathways, how to diagnose, carry out assessments and draw up appropriate treatment plan to meet specific needs.
	Ensure that after discharge client is transferred to a robust aftercare plan for proper follow up and management.


Requirements


	A minimum of Master's Degree in Clinical Psychology
	Minimum of 2 years Clinical experience
	Business Acumen and Business development experience
	Strong verbal and written communication skill
	Proactive problem-solving approach
	High level of professionalism
	Ability to uphold strict confidentiality
	Ability to observe, assess, and record symptoms, reactions, and progress.
	Knowledge of crisis intervention techniques.
	Ability to effectively communicate medical information, test results, diagnoses and/or proposed treatment in a manner easily understood by the client.
	Interviewing and psychological/developmental evaluation skills
	Knowledge of psychological test administration, scoring, and interpretation.
	Ability to maintain emotional stability to cope with human suffering, emergencies, and other stresses.


Salary
N200,000.00 - N250,000.00 / Month (Gross)

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position and location as the subject of the email.
